reasoning: First step: ingest all patient PDFs

demographics.text:
ADMISSION NOTE
==============
Patient Name: Robert Okafor
MRN: MRN-334556
Date of Birth: 11/28/1945
Age: 78 years old
Gender: Male

Admission Date: 05/09/2024

ADMITTING DIAGNOSIS: Acute Exacerbation of COPD

ALLERGIES: NKDA

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Patient presents with acute exacerbation of copd.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
78M with COPD presenting with 3 days worsening dyspnea and productive cough. CXR showed RLL infiltrate consistent with CAP. Started on IV steroids, bronchodilators, and azithromycin. Cardiology consulted for new AF with RVR � amiodarone started for rate control. INR supratherapeutic at 3.8 on admission; warfarin held x2 days then restarted. Improved clinically; O2 requirement decreased.

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS:
- Warfarin 5 mg Daily
- Digoxin 0.125 mg Daily
- Metoprolol Succinate 50 mg Daily
- Furosemide 40 mg Daily
- Tiotropium 18 mcg Daily
- Albuterol 2.5 mg Q4H PRN


VITAL SIGNS ON ADMISSION:
Blood Pressure: 138/88 mmHg
Heart Rate: 96 bpm
Temperature: 37.8 C
Respiratory Rate: 18/min
O2 Saturation: 97% on room air

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Alert and oriented x3. Mildly distressed.
Pertinent findings per admitting note.

PLAN:
- Chest X-ray (RLL infiltrate)
- CT chest without contrast
- Sputum collection for culture
- Echocardiogram (EF 35%)

Signed: Dr. [Admitting Physician]

PROGRESS NOTE � Day 2
======================
Patient: Robert Okafor    MRN: MRN-334556
Date: 05/10/2024

SUBJECTIVE:
Patient reports improved symptoms. Tolerating medications.

OBJECTIVE:
Vitals: BP 132/80, HR 88, Temp 37.2, RR 16, O2 Sat 98% RA

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Discharge Diagnosis: Acute Exacerbation of COPD, Community-Acquired Pneumonia

#1. Atrial Fibrillation
#2. Heart Failure with reduced EF (HFrEF)
#3. Type 2 Diabetes

Plan:
- Continue current medications
- Monitor labs
- Sputum culture � pending (ordered 05/09)
- INR (draw 05/15) � pending, warfarin dose adjustment required once available


Signed: Dr. [Covering Physician]

DISCHARGE SUMMARY
=================
Patient Name: Robert Okafor
MRN: MRN-334556
Date of Birth: 11/28/1945
Admission Date: 05/09/2024
Discharge Date: 05/14/2024

PRINCIPAL DIAGNOSIS: Acute Exacerbation of COPD, Community-Acquired Pneumonia

SECONDARY DIAGNOSES:
- Atrial Fibrillation
- Heart Failure with reduced EF (HFrEF)
- Type 2 Diabetes


DISCHARGE CONDITION: Improved

ALLERGIES: NKDA

HOSPITAL COURSE:
78M with COPD presenting with 3 days worsening dyspnea and productive cough. CXR showed RLL infiltrate consistent with CAP. Started on IV steroids, bronchodilators, and azithromycin. Cardiology consulted for new AF with RVR � amiodarone started for rate control. INR supratherapeutic at 3.8 on admission; warfarin held x2 days then restarted. Improved clinically; O2 requirement decreased.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
- Chest X-ray (RLL infiltrate)
- CT chest without contrast
- Sputum collection for culture
- Echocardiogram (EF 35%)


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
- Warfarin 5 mg PO Daily
- Amiodarone 200 mg PO Daily
- Digoxin 0.125 mg PO Daily
- Metoprolol Succinate 50 mg PO Daily
- Furosemide 40 mg PO Daily
- Tiotropium 18 mcg Inhaled Daily
- Albuterol 2.5 mg Neb Q4H PRN
- Azithromycin 250 mg PO Daily x5 days
- Prednisone 40 mg PO Daily x5 days


PENDING RESULTS AT DISCHARGE:
- Sputum culture � pending (ordered 05/09) (PENDING)
- INR (draw 05/15) � pending, warfarin dose adjustment required once available (PENDING)


FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
- Pulmonology follow-up in 2 weeks
- Cardiology follow-up in 1 week � INR check and amiodarone monitoring
- Repeat CXR in 4 weeks
- Resume Warfarin per home dose; INR to be checked by PCP within 3 days


Signed: Dr. [Discharging Physician]

LABORATORY RESULTS
==================
Patient: Robert Okafor    MRN: MRN-334556

COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT (Admission):
WBC:        11.2  [4.5-11.0]  H
Hemoglobin: 12.8  [12.0-16.0]
Hematocrit: 38.5  [36.0-48.0]
Platelets:  245   [150-400]

COMPREHENSIVE METABOLIC PANEL:
Sodium:     138   [136-145]
Potassium:  3.2   [3.5-5.1]   L
Chloride:   101   [98-107]
CO2:        18    [22-29]     L
BUN:        24    [7-20]      H
Creatinine: 1.4   [0.6-1.2]   H
Glucose:    412   [70-100]    H
eGFR:       48    mL/min      (CKD Stage 3)

PENDING RESULTS:
Sputum culture � pending (ordered 05/09)
INR (draw 05/15) � pending, warfarin dose adjustment required once available


Report generated by: Laboratory Information System

MEDICATION ADMINISTRATION RECORD
==================================
Patient: Robert Okafor    MRN: MRN-334556
Period: 05/09/2024 to 05/14/2024

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS (Home Medications on Admission):
Warfarin  5 mg  PO  Daily
Digoxin  0.125 mg  PO  Daily
Metoprolol Succinate  50 mg  PO  Daily
Furosemide  40 mg  PO  Daily
Tiotropium  18 mcg  Inhaled  Daily
Albuterol  2.5 mg  Neb  Q4H PRN


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
Warfarin  5 mg  PO  Daily
Amiodarone  200 mg  PO  Daily
Digoxin  0.125 mg  PO  Daily
Metoprolol Succinate  50 mg  PO  Daily
Furosemide  40 mg  PO  Daily
Tiotropium  18 mcg  Inhaled  Daily
Albuterol  2.5 mg  Neb  Q4H PRN
Azithromycin  250 mg  PO  Daily x5 days
Prednisone  40 mg  PO  Daily x5 days

78M with COPD presenting with 3 days worsening dyspnea and productive cough. CXR showed RLL infiltrate consistent with CAP. Started on IV steroids, bronchodilators, and azithromycin. Cardiology consulted for new AF with RVR � amiodarone started for rate control. INR supratherapeutic at 3.8 on admission; warfarin held x2 days then restarted. Improved clinically; O2 requirement decreased.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
- Chest X-ray (RLL infiltrate)
- CT chest without contrast
- Sputum collection for culture
- Echocardiogram (EF 35%)


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
- Warfarin 5 mg PO Daily
- Amiodarone 200 mg PO Daily
- Digoxin 0.125 mg PO Daily
- Metoprolol Succinate 50 mg PO Daily
- Furosemide 40 mg PO Daily
- Tiotropium 18 mcg Inhaled Daily
- Albuterol 2.5 mg Neb Q4H PRN
- Azithromycin 250 mg PO Daily x5 days
- Prednisone 40 mg PO Daily x5 days


PENDING RESULTS AT DISCHARGE:
- Sputum culture � pending (ordered 05/09) (PENDING)
- INR (draw 05/15) � pending, warfarin dose adjustment required once available (PENDING)


FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
- Pulmonology follow-up in 2 weeks
- Cardiology follow-up in 1 week � INR check and amiodarone monitoring
- Repeat CXR in 4 weeks
- Resume Warfarin per home dose; INR to be checked by PCP within 3 days


Signed: Dr. [Discharging Physician]
